scope.goal: Map the direct caller graph around locale constructor 0x1401868F0, its sibling bad-locale/default-locale constructors, and the reusable Reverser decoder gaps exposed by those windows.
scope.relationship_to_previous_milestone: This follows the prior slot-table/locale consumer milestone upward from constructor 0x1401868F0 into every direct caller in this PE image, plus three sibling constructors that share the bad-locale literal, generation counters, and descriptor-row patterns.

locale_constructor_0x1401868f0_direct_ca.role: The direct rel32 caller set for the previously identified locale/name constructor is now fully enumerated by the native direct-call scanner.
locale_constructor_0x1401868f0_direct_ca.confidence: high for the direct call graph and callsite ownership; medium for exact standard-library class or facet names.
supporting_evidence: All four caller functions decode with zero unknown instructions after the byte-form decoder updates.
The broader sibling set shares references to 0x140C558D0, 0x140B6A720, and locale/facet descriptor rows.

wrapper_0x140186b30.role: Small wrapper around 0x1401868F0 that brackets locale construction with virtual/facet callbacks and returns a byte-like status.
observed_behavior: Loads a callback/facet object from [RCX+0x40]+0x08 and calls qword ptr [RAX+0x08] before locale construction.
Calls 0x1401868F0 at 0x140186B58 with a stack-local output object and the incoming boolean-like DL value.
Dispatches through qword ptr [R8+0x40], qword ptr [RDX+0x10], and qword ptr [R8] after construction when optional objects are non-null.
Returns a MOVZX-expanded byte result in EAX.

numeric_or_facet_locale_path_0x1404790d0.role: Locale/facet construction wrapper that normalizes sign and base-prefix state, calls 0x1401868F0, then activates or queries facet callbacks.
numeric_or_facet_locale_path_0x1404790d0.confidence: medium-high for sign/base-prefix parsing and locale constructor ownership; medium for exact numeric/facet class name.
observed_behavior: Checks the first input byte for plus/minus by decoding SUB AL, 0x2B followed by TEST AL, 0xFD.
Checks for a 0x or 0X prefix by comparing a leading zero and decoding SUB AL, 0x58 followed by TEST AL, 0xDF.
Masks [R9+0x18] with 0xE00 and compares it to 0x800 before choosing one parse/facet path.
Calls 0x1401868F0 after callback slot [RAX+0x08], then performs multiple virtual calls at [RDX+0x10], [R8], [RAX+0x38], [RAX+0x28], and [RAX+0x20].

expanded_numeric_or_facet_locale_path_0x.role: Larger sibling of 0x1404790D0 that builds temporary string/storage state, calls the locale constructor, and repeatedly dispatches through facet slots.
expanded_numeric_or_facet_locale_path_0x.confidence: medium-high for relationship to 0x1404790D0 due to matching sign/base-prefix logic and shared call tail; medium for exact class name.
observed_behavior: Reuses the same plus/minus and 0x or 0X prefix checks observed in 0x1404790D0.
Allocates or prepares temporary state through 0x1407CD760 and 0x1407CDB30 before calling 0x1401868F0.
Writes a dot byte/word value into a stack-local small-string style buffer before calling allocation/copy helpers.
Performs a long sequence of indirect calls through [RAX+0x18], suggesting repeated facet/object slot processing over a sequence of prepared records.
References literal/data targets 0x140B88B9C and 0x140B88BA0 from the early setup path; their exact semantic labels remain pending.

regex_or_pattern_locale_owner_0x1407a5cd.role: Higher-level owner that references pattern literal '(.*)', calls the bad-locale sibling 0x1407A8D80, and then calls locale constructor 0x1401868F0.
regex_or_pattern_locale_owner_0x1407a5cd.confidence: medium-high for regex/pattern ownership due to literal '(.*)' and calls into the 0x1407A8xxx cluster; medium for exact subsystem name.

important_globals.0x140C558D0: Shared generation counter also used by 0x1401868F0 and 0x14047A480.
important_globals.0x140E93BA0: Virtual-only .data current/default object slot loaded and stored in this function.
important_globals.0x140E9C840: Virtual-only .data state slot loaded, compared, stored, and reloaded in this function.

bad_locale_and_default_locale_sibling_co.role: Three sibling constructor/default-initializer paths reuse the same locale-name parse/finalize helpers and converge on the bad-locale throw helper 0x1407B57BC.

0x14047A480..0x14047A68A.role: Default/current locale constructor path using descriptor row 0x140806AB8 and globals 0x140E93AF0/0x140E98070.
0x14047A480..0x14047A68A.literal: bad locale name at 0x140B6A720 referenced from 0x14047A67D

0x14047AA60..0x14047ACBC.role: Bool/numeric text facet-style constructor path using descriptor row 0x140806B18 and literals false/true plus bad-locale handling.
literals: bad locale name at 0x140B6A720 referenced from 0x14047ACA3

0x1407A8D80..0x1407A8FDB.role: Regex/pattern-adjacent locale constructor path using descriptor row 0x140806BB8 and called by 0x1407A5CD0.
0x1407A8D80..0x1407A8FDB.literal: bad locale name at 0x140B6A720 referenced from 0x1407A8FCE

shared_helpers.0x1407B6098: Called by 0x1401868F0, 0x14047A480, 0x14047AA60, and 0x1407A8D80; likely locale-name parse/copy setup.
shared_helpers.0x1407B6104: Called by the same four constructor paths; likely finalize/normalize step before cleanup.
shared_helpers.0x1407B63A8: Shared guard/default-state helper with 15 observed direct callsites in the scanned targets.
shared_helpers.0x1407B57BC: Bad-locale throw/report helper with four direct callsites tied to the literal 'bad locale name'.

multibyte_conversion_bridge_0x1407a8c5e.role: Small bridge into the previously mapped multibyte conversion helper 0x1407B78E0.
multibyte_conversion_bridge_0x1407a8c5e.note: This keeps 0x140479C90 and 0x14047BAC0 on the worklist because they likely bridge the locale/facet constructor family to the multibyte conversion path.

globals.0x140C558D0: Generation counter with eight observed references across 0x1401868F0, 0x14047A480, 0x14047A690, and 0x1407A5CD0.
globals.0x140C558E8: Cached generation/current-id pointer used by 0x1401868F0 only in this scan.
globals.0x140E93700: Virtual-only .data current/default object pointer loaded and stored by 0x1401868F0.

decoder_gaps_closed: Accumulator-immediate byte forms 04/0C/14/1C/24/2C/34/3C, including SUB AL, 0x2B and CMP AL, 0x1F.
Common byte ModRM arithmetic/logical forms, including ADD [RDI+0xBF], AL and REX-prefixed XOR SIL, SIL.
FE /0 and FE /1 byte INC/DEC, including DEC AL.
tooling_updates.why_it_matters: The caller windows initially misaligned around byte-form prefix checks and reported DB fallbacks. After the tool update, all eight windows decode with zero unknown instructions and the sign/base-prefix checks are visible in native Reverser output.

validation_notes.qword_warning_explanation: Expected for virtual-only .data readback at 0x140E93700, 0x140E93AF0, 0x140E93BA0, 0x140E98070, and 0x140E9C840; RIP-relative address references for those globals remain valid.

next_targets: Map 0x1407B6098 and 0x1407B6104 to prove the locale-name parse/finalize lifecycle shared by all four constructor families.
Map 0x1407B57BC enough to name the bad-locale throw/report path and distinguish exception construction from error logging.
Trace descriptor row slots for 0x140806AB8, 0x140806B18, and 0x140806BB8, especially slot0 functions 0x140478F20, 0x140479C30, and 0x1407A8FE0.
Follow 0x14047A690, 0x14047B9E0, 0x14047A900, 0x14047B290, 0x14047BAC0, and 0x140479C90 to connect the caller graph to facet activation and multibyte conversion.
Trace the direct callers of 0x14047A480 outside this window cluster, especially 0x140477F5A, 0x14047812A, and the 0x1404A69xx group, to tie default-locale setup back into client bootstrap ownership.
